#  Welcome to the Mayor’s Office 
![](https://www.nyc.gov/adobe/dynamicmedia/deliver/dm-aid--7013d7cb-03c2-4069-a9a5-91c5d9e57444/Mayors-office-one-optimized-jpg.webp?preferwebp=true)
![](https://www.nyc.gov/adobe/dynamicmedia/deliver/dm-aid--2c8489a5-e4eb-4ff5-8c50-00507eec5fc0/zohran-mamdani.jpg?preferwebp=true&quality=85)
## Zohran Kwame Mamdani 
#### 112th Mayor of New York City
Zohran Mamdani was sworn in as Mayor of the City of New York on January 1st, 2026. Prior to becoming Mayor, he represented the 36th New York State Assembly District and its neighborhoods of Astoria, Ditmars-Steinway, and Astoria Heights.
## Latest News

#  Mayor Mamdani Takes Bold, Unapologetic Actions to Protect New Yorkers in First 100 Days 
April 7, 2026
* * *
## What you should know
  * From doubling down on Sanctuary City policies to committing firmly to closing Rikers, Mamdani started his administration with the most vulnerable in mind


* * *
**NEW YORK** — In his first 100 days in office, New York City Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani moved swiftly to deliver on his promise to protect New Yorkers, advancing policies centered on health, safety, and dignity — especially for those most often left behind.
“Protecting New Yorkers demands action rooted in justice, equity and care,” said **New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani**. “In our first 100 days, we took concrete steps to increase public health and safety, protect vulnerable and historically disenfranchised communities, and reaffirm New York as a place of refuge and possibility.”
**Advancing True Public Safety**
Mayor Mamdani advanced a comprehensive approach to public safety — one that prioritizes prevention, transparency and fairness. Actions include: 
  * Driving crime to historically low levels in the first three months, Driving crime to historically low levels in the first three months, including record-low incidents of murder and shootings. 
  * Appointing the City’s first Deputy Mayor for Community Safety and creating the first-ever Office of Community Safety 
  * Codifying the release of body-worn camera footage within 30 days of critical incidents, strengthening accountability 
  * Ending criminal enforcement for low-level traffic offenses involving e-bike riders and cyclists 


**Protecting Immigrant New Yorkers**
In the face of escalating federal threats, the Mamdani administration acted decisively to defend immigrant communities and uphold New York’s legacy as a sanctuary city. Actions include: 
  * Launching a Know Your Rights campaign in 10 languages and distributing 30,000 flyers through houses of worship 
  * Signing Executive Order 13 to reinforce sanctuary protections by: 
    * Prohibiting ICE from entering City properties — including schools, shelters, and hospitals — without a judicial warrant 
    * Strengthening data privacy protections 
    * Ordering agency audits and creating a crisis-response task force 


**Reforming the Corrections System**
The Mamdani Administration committed to closing Rikers Island while advancing immediate reforms to ensure humane conditions for those in custody and those who work in the system. Actions include:
  * Ordering full compliance with the City’s ban on solitary confinement and Board of Correction minimum standards 
  * Appointing Stanley Richards as Department of Correction commissioner, the first formerly incarcerated person to lead the department 
  * Opening the first Outposted Therapeutic Housing Unit at NYC Health + Hospitals/Bellevue, expanding access to specialized care for the most clinically vulnerable at Rikers Island. 


**Responding to Severe Winter Storms**
During one of the harshest winters in recent years, the administration mobilized a large-scale response to keep New Yorkers safe and the city operational. Actions include: 
  * Expanding NotifyNYC to nearly 1.5 million subscribers 
  * Implementing the first-ever 24-hour Code Blue, opening new warming centers, deploying new warming buses and transitioning approximately 2,000 placements of unsheltered New Yorkers into shelter between January 19 and March 4 


**Keeping New Yorkers Healthy**
The Mamdani Administration prioritized access to care and services that allow all New Yorkers to live with dignity. Actions include: 
  * Closing the dilapidated 30th Street Shelter, while maintaining overall shelter capacity 
  * Opening the City’s first-ever pet inclusive family shelter at Magnolia Gardens and a new HELP Women’s Intake Shelter in East New York 
  * Investing $20 million in perinatal and early childhood mental health through the Strong Foundations Initiative 
  * Launching a $1 million public campaign to promote vaccinations 
  * Expanding overdose prevention services and opening new youth health clinics in Brooklyn and Queens 


**Protecting LGBTQIA+ New Yorkers**
Mayor Mamdani established the Office of LGBTQIA+ Affairs to lead policies and programs supporting LGBTQIA+ New Yorkers.  
He appointed civil rights attorney Taylor Brown — the first openly transgender person to lead a New York City agency — to head the office, marking a historic step toward inclusive leadership.

**Zohran Kwame Mamdani**[[c]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-14) (born October 18, 1991) is an American politician who has served as the 112th [mayor of New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayor_of_New_York_City "Mayor of New York City") since January 2026. A member of the [Democratic Party](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_Party_\(United_States\) "Democratic Party \(United States\)") and the [Democratic Socialists of America](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_Socialists_of_America "Democratic Socialists of America"), Mamdani served from 2021 to 2025 as a member of the [New York State Assembly](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_State_Assembly "New York State Assembly") for the [36th district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York%27s_36th_State_Assembly_district "New York's 36th State Assembly district"), representing [Astoria, Queens](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astoria,_Queens "Astoria, Queens"). 
Mamdani was born in [Kampala](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kampala "Kampala") to Indian parents, academic [Mahmood Mamdani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahmood_Mamdani "Mahmood Mamdani") and filmmaker [Mira Nair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mira_Nair "Mira Nair"). When he was seven years old, after having spent three years in [Cape Town](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cape_Town "Cape Town"), he and his family moved to [New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). Mamdani graduated from the [Bronx High School of Science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bronx_High_School_of_Science "Bronx High School of Science") before receiving a bachelor's degree with a major in [Africana studies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africana_studies "Africana studies") from [Bowdoin College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bowdoin_College "Bowdoin College") in 2014. 
After working as a housing counselor and [rapper](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rapper "Rapper"), Mamdani entered [New York City politics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_politics "New York City politics") as a campaign manager for [Khader El-Yateem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khader_El-Yateem "Khader El-Yateem") and [Ross Barkan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ross_Barkan "Ross Barkan"). He was first elected to the [New York State Assembly](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_State_Assembly "New York State Assembly") in 2020, defeating five-term incumbent [Aravella Simotas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aravella_Simotas "Aravella Simotas") in the Democratic primary. Representing Astoria and [Long Island City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Long_Island_City "Long Island City"), he was reelected without opposition in 2022 and 2024. 
In October 2024, Mamdani announced his candidacy for mayor of New York City in the [2025 election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election "2025 New York City mayoral election"). A [democratic socialist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_socialism "Democratic socialism"), Mamdani campaigned on a [progressive](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Progressivism_in_the_United_States "Progressivism in the United States"), affordability-focused platform, supporting [fare-free city buses](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fare-free_city_buses "Fare-free city buses"), universal [child care](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Child_care "Child care"), [city-owned grocery stores](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/City-owned_grocery_stores "City-owned grocery stores"), a rent freeze on rent-stabilized units, additional affordable housing units, and a $30 [minimum wage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minimum_wage_in_the_United_States "Minimum wage in the United States") by 2030. He also expressed support for [LGBTQ rights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LGBTQ_rights_in_the_United_States "LGBTQ rights in the United States"), comprehensive [public safety reform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Police_reform_in_the_United_States "Police reform in the United States"), and tax increases on corporations and those earning above [$1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High-net-worth_individual "High-net-worth individual"). He won [the Democratic primary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_Democratic_mayoral_primary "2025 New York City Democratic mayoral primary") in June 2025, defeating former governor [Andrew Cuomo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Cuomo "Andrew Cuomo") in an upset, and was elected mayor in [the November general election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election "2025 New York City mayoral election"). He is New York City's first [Muslim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muslims "Muslims") and first [Asian American](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asian_Americans "Asian Americans") mayor. 
## Early life and education
Zohran Kwame Mamdani was born on October 18, 1991, in [Kampala](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kampala "Kampala"), Uganda, the only child of postcolonialist academic [Mahmood Mamdani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahmood_Mamdani "Mahmood Mamdani") and filmmaker [Mira Nair](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mira_Nair "Mira Nair").[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kitunzi-2025-15)[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Marcelo-2025-16) He was given his middle name, [Kwame](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kwame "Kwame"), by his father in honor of [Kwame Nkrumah](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kwame_Nkrumah "Kwame Nkrumah"), the first [president of Ghana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_Ghana "President of Ghana").[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17)[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Davids-2024-18) Both his parents are of [Indian descent](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_people "Indian people").[[16]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Roy2025-19) His father is a [Gujarati Muslim](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gujarati_Muslim "Gujarati Muslim") who was born in [Mumbai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai") and raised in Uganda.[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Chen-2016-20) His mother is a [Punjabi Hindu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Punjabi_Hindu "Punjabi Hindu")[[18]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-21)[[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-22)[[20]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-23) who was born in [Rourkela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rourkela "Rourkela") and raised in [Bhubaneswar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bhubaneswar "Bhubaneswar").[[21]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-24) His paternal grandparents were born in present-day [Tanzania](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tanzania "Tanzania"), and his father's family was part of the [Indian diaspora in Southeast Africa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_diaspora_in_Southeast_Africa "Indian diaspora in Southeast Africa").[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Chen-2016-20) His maternal grandfather, Amrit Lal Nair, was a former [Indian Administrative Service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_Administrative_Service "Indian Administrative Service") (IAS) officer,[[22]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-25) and his maternal grandmother, Praveen Nair, was a [social worker](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_work "Social work") and founder of the [Salaam Baalak Trust](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Salaam_Baalak_Trust "Salaam Baalak Trust") in India.[[23]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-26)
Mamdani lived in Kampala until the age of five, when his family moved to [Cape Town, South Africa](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cape_Town,_South_Africa "Cape Town, South Africa"),[[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kitunzi-2025-15) after his father was appointed head of [African studies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/African_studies "African studies") at the [University of Cape Town](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Cape_Town "University of Cape Town").[[24]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-lahr2002-27) He attended [St. George's Grammar School](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._George%27s_Grammar_School_\(Cape_Town\) "St. George's Grammar School \(Cape Town\)") in [Mowbray](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mowbray,_Cape_Town "Mowbray, Cape Town") from 1996 to 1998,[[15]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Davids-2024-18)[[25]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-28) during the early [post-apartheid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Post-apartheid "Post-apartheid") years. He later said that the experience of living in Cape Town "taught me what inequality looks like up close[[26]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-mtembu2025-29)
The family moved to the United States and settled in New York City when Mamdani was seven,[[27]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-30) and he was raised in Manhattan's [Morningside Heights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Morningside_Heights "Morningside Heights") neighborhood.[[28]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Haag-2025-31) He has called his upbringing "privileged", saying, "I never had to want for something, and yet I knew that was not in any way the reality for most New Yorkers."[[29]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-32) As a child, he was often present on his mother's film sets, where he was loved by members of the film crews, who variously called him "Z", "Zoru", "Fadoose", and "Nonstop Mamdani".[[24]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-lahr2002-27)
Mamdani attended the [Bank Street School for Children](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bank_Street_College_of_Education#Bank_Street_School_for_Children "Bank Street College of Education") on the [Upper West Side](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Upper_West_Side "Upper West Side") of Manhattan, where he successfully ran as the independent candidate in a middle school [mock election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mock_election "Mock election"), adopting a platform of "equal rights, anti-war policies that proposed spending money on education rather than the military".[[30]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-33) In 2003, he returned to Kampala for a year and attended school during his father's [sabbatical](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sabbatical "Sabbatical") there;[[17]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Chen-2016-20) his paternal grandparents and aunt still lived there and helped take care of him while his father was working on the book _Good Muslim, Bad Muslim_.[[31]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-34)
In 2010, Mamdani graduated from the [Bronx High School of Science](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bronx_High_School_of_Science "Bronx High School of Science") in [Kingsbridge Heights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kingsbridge_Heights "Kingsbridge Heights"), [Bronx](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bronx "Bronx"),[[32]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-35)[[33]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-36)[[34]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Saltonstall-2025-37) where he co-founded the school's first [cricket](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") team[[13]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Marcelo-2025-16) and unsuccessfully ran for student body vice president.[[35]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-38) He also played soccer with the West Side Soccer League.[[34]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Saltonstall-2025-37)
Mamdani attended [Bowdoin College](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bowdoin_College "Bowdoin College") in [Brunswick, Maine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brunswick,_Maine "Brunswick, Maine"), where he co-founded the school's chapter of [Students for Justice in Palestine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Students_for_Justice_in_Palestine "Students for Justice in Palestine").[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Shoki-2020-39)[[37]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Sorkin-2019-40) He was a regular contributor to the campus newspaper _[The Bowdoin Orient](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bowdoin_Orient "The Bowdoin Orient")_ , covering politics, culture, and sports in his columns, "Kwame's Kolumnalu" and "Talk of the Quad".[[38]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-41) In January 2014, he co-authored an [op-ed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Op-ed "Op-ed") in the _[Bangor Daily News](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangor_Daily_News "Bangor Daily News")_ urging Bowdoin to join the [American Studies Association's boycott of Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/American_Studies_Association%27s_boycott_of_Israel "American Studies Association's boycott of Israel") and criticizing the college's president, [Barry Mills](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Mills_\(college_president\) "Barry Mills \(college president\)").[[39]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-42) Mamdani graduated from Bowdoin in 2014 with a bachelor's degree in [Africana studies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Africana_studies "Africana studies").[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Shoki-2020-39)[[37]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Sorkin-2019-40)
## Early career
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fd/Zohran_Mamdani_on_TMTv-SA.jpg/250px-Zohran_Mamdani_on_TMTv-SA.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Zohran_Mamdani_on_TMTv-SA.jpg)Mamdani at the 2016 Johannesburg premiere of _Queen of Katwe_
Mamdani is a fan of [hip-hop](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hip-hop "Hip-hop") and has composed, performed, and produced rap music.[[40]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Green-2025-43) Under the moniker Young Cardamom, he collaborated with his best friend, Ugandan rapper HAB (Abdul Bar Hussein), whose origins are in [South Sudan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Sudan "South Sudan"),[[41]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-brara-44)[[42]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-rap2015-45) as Young Cardamom & HAB. Their first song, "Kanda [Chap Chap]", was about [chapati](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chapati "Chapati"), an [Indian flatbread](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_flatbread "Indian flatbread").[[43]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Onmanorama2025-46) They performed tracks from their 2016 [EP](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/EP "EP") _Sidda Mukyaalo_ ("No going back to the village") at the [Nyege Nyege](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nyege_Nyege "Nyege Nyege") festival.[[44]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-47)[[45]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Waswa-2025-48) The pair rapped in languages including [Nubi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nubi "Nubi"), [Luganda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luganda "Luganda"), [Swahili](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Swahili "Swahili"), and English, partly to create a unique Ugandan style of rap rather than imitating American rap, and partly to convey that Ugandan residents with roots in other countries are all Ugandan. The chapati was chosen as a symbol because it originates in the [Indian subcontinent](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_subcontinent "Indian subcontinent") but has become a Ugandan staple.[[42]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-rap2015-45) In their music, they addressed social issues in Uganda, such as corruption and "black and brown relations", as well as [colonialism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colonisation_of_Africa "Colonisation of Africa").[[46]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-int2016-49) Young Cardamom & HAB were nominated for "Rookie of the Year" at the inaugural Ugandan (UG) Hip Hop Awards.[[47]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-50)[[48]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-51)
Mamdani curated and produced [the soundtrack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queen_of_Katwe_\(Original_Motion_Picture_Soundtrack\) "Queen of Katwe \(Original Motion Picture Soundtrack\)") for his mother Mira Nair's 2016 film _[Queen of Katwe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queen_of_Katwe "Queen of Katwe")_ , for which he was nominated for a 2017 [Guild of Music Supervisors Awards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guild_of_Music_Supervisors_Awards "Guild of Music Supervisors Awards");[[49]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-52)[[50]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-53)[[51]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-disney-54) he co-wrote the song "#1 Spice" with HAB for the film.[[41]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-brara-44)[[52]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-55) Mamdani also appears as an extra in the film,[[53]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-tusing2025-56) and is credited as [third assistant director](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Third_assistant_director "Third assistant director").[[51]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-disney-54) Nair subsequently offered him parts in the stage musical adaptation of her film _[Monsoon Wedding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monsoon_Wedding "Monsoon Wedding")_ , after he took part in [stage readings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stage_reading "Stage reading") for the show, and her [television adaptation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A_Suitable_Boy_\(TV_series\) "A Suitable Boy \(TV series\)") of the [Vikram Seth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vikram_Seth "Vikram Seth") novel _[A Suitable Boy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/A_Suitable_Boy "A Suitable Boy")_ , but he declined.[[54]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-57)[[55]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-58)
In 2017, Mamdani released the song "Salaam" under the name Zohran Kwame.[[56]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-59) In the song, Mamdani praised the Holy Land Five, five people arrested and connected to the [Holy Land Foundation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Holy_Land_Foundation_for_Relief_and_Development "Holy Land Foundation for Relief and Development").[[57]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-60)[[58]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-61) In April 2019, under the moniker Mr. Cardamom, he released the single "Nani", an homage to his grandmother. Actress and food writer [Madhur Jaffrey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Madhur_Jaffrey "Madhur Jaffrey") portrays Mamdani's grandmother in the video,[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-rayner2019-62)[[60]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-63)[[61]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-64)[[62]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-65) which pays tribute to Jaffrey and [New York's South Asian culture](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asians_in_New_York_City "Asians in New York City").[[59]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-rayner2019-62)
Mamdani entered [New York City politics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_politics "New York City politics") as a volunteer for Ali Najmi's campaign in the [2015 special election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City%27s_23rd_City_Council_district#2015_special "New York City's 23rd City Council district") for the [23rd district](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City%27s_23rd_City_Council_district "New York City's 23rd City Council district") of the City Council.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17) Mamdani was inspired to join Najmi's campaign after learning that he was supported by [Heems](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heems "Heems"), a New York rapper of Indian descent and co-founder of alternative hip-hop group [Das Racist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Das_Racist "Das Racist").[[40]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Green-2025-43)[[63]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-66) Specifically, Mamdani attributes his involvement in local politics to a 2015 _[The Village Voice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Village_Voice "The Village Voice")_ article about Najmi and Heems, whom he described as one of his favorite rappers.[[64]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-67)[[65]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-68)
In 2017, Mamdani joined the New York City chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA) and worked for the campaign of [New York City Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_Council "New York City Council") candidate [Khader El-Yateem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Khader_El-Yateem "Khader El-Yateem"), a [Palestinian Lutheran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palestinian_Christians "Palestinian Christians") [minister](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minister_\(Christianity\) "Minister \(Christianity\)") and democratic socialist from [Bay Ridge, Brooklyn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bay_Ridge,_Brooklyn "Bay Ridge, Brooklyn").[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17) Part of Mamdani's motivation for joining the DSA was its pro-Palestine stance, which aligned with his own prior activism.[[66]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-69) Mamdani served as the campaign manager for [Ross Barkan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ross_Barkan "Ross Barkan")'s 2018 unsuccessful bid for the [New York State Senate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_State_Senate "New York State Senate") and was also a field organizer for fellow democratic socialist [Tiffany Cabán](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tiffany_Cab%C3%A1n "Tiffany Cabán")'s close-run but also unsuccessful 2019 campaign for [Queens County district attorney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queens_County_district_attorney "Queens County district attorney").[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17)[[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Mamdani-2021-70)[[68]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-71)
Starting in 2018,[[69]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-72) Mamdani worked as a [foreclosure](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foreclosure "Foreclosure") prevention and housing counselor. There, he assisted lower-income immigrant homeowners in [Queens](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queens "Queens") with [eviction notices](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eviction_notice "Eviction notice") and efforts to prevent them from being evicted from their homes. He said the experience motivated him to run for office to address the housing and affordability crisis.[[70]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-73)
Further information: [Electoral history of Zohran Mamdani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electoral_history_of_Zohran_Mamdani "Electoral history of Zohran Mamdani")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/Zohran_Mamdani_at_the_Resist_Fascism_Rally_in_Bryant_Park_on_Oct_27th_2024.jpg/250px-Zohran_Mamdani_at_the_Resist_Fascism_Rally_in_Bryant_Park_on_Oct_27th_2024.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Zohran_Mamdani_at_the_Resist_Fascism_Rally_in_Bryant_Park_on_Oct_27th_2024.jpg)Mamdani at the Resist Fascism Rally in [Bryant Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bryant_Park "Bryant Park"), October 27, 2024
In October 2019, Mamdani announced his campaign to represent New York's 36th State Assembly district, which encompasses Astoria and [Long Island City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Long_Island_City "Long Island City") in Queens.[[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kaufman-2020-74)[[72]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-75) He was endorsed by the DSA,[[73]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-76) running on a platform of [housing reform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Right_to_housing "Right to housing"), [police](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Police_reform_in_the_United_States "Police reform in the United States") and [prison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prisoner_rights_in_the_United_States "Prisoner rights in the United States") reform, and [public ownership of utilities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_utility "Public utility").[[71]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kaufman-2020-74) Mamdani's June 2020 [primary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Primary_election "Primary election") victory over five-term Democratic incumbent [Aravella Simotas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aravella_Simotas "Aravella Simotas") took almost a month to call,[[74]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-77) and he won the general election with no [Republican](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Republican_Party_\(United_States\) "Republican Party \(United States\)") opposition in November.[[75]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-78) Mamdani was reelected without opposition in 2022 and 2024.[[76]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-79)[[77]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-80)
Mamdani was a member of the DSA's nine-member[[d]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-81) ["State Socialists in Office" bloc](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_State_Socialists_in_Office "New York State Socialists in Office") in New York and a member of the Muslim Democratic Club of New York.[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17)[[78]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-82)[[79]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Sterne-2024-83) He was the keynote speaker at the 2023 DSA convention, saying, "We are special as DSA electeds not because of ourselves; we are special because of our organization".[[80]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-84)[[81]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-85)[[82]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-86)
By January 2025, Mamdani was a member of nine Assembly committees: the Committee on Aging; the Committee on Cities; the Committee on Election Law; the Committee on Energy; the Committee on Real Property Taxation; the Black, Puerto Rican, Hispanic & Asian Legislative Caucus; the Puerto Rican/Hispanic Task Force; the Asian Pacific American Task Force; and the Task Force on New Americans.[[83]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-87)
By May 2025, Mamdani had been the primary sponsor of 20 bills in the Assembly—three of which became law—and the co-sponsor of 238 bills.[[84]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-88) As a member of the Assembly, he helped launch a successful fare-free bus pilot program and participated in a [hunger strike](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hunger_strike "Hunger strike") alongside taxi drivers.[[85]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-89)[[86]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-90) Due to his campaign, Mamdani had the most absences from the Assembly session of any Democrat, and the second-most overall, after [David McDonough](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_McDonough "David McDonough").[[87]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-91)
## Mayor of New York City
### 2025 campaign
Main articles: [2025 New York City Democratic mayoral primary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_Democratic_mayoral_primary "2025 New York City Democratic mayoral primary") and [2025 New York City mayoral election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election "2025 New York City mayoral election")
On October 23, 2024, Mamdani announced his candidacy for [mayor of New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayor_of_New_York_City "Mayor of New York City").[[88]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-92) His platform included support for free city buses and a rent freeze in rent-stabilized housing.[[89]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Mays-2025-93)[[90]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-94) Mamdani also wants the city government to operate five [grocery stores](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grocery_store "Grocery store")—one in each borough—to drive down grocery prices.[[91]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-95) His platform included support for universal child care and the construction of 200,000 new affordable housing units.[[92]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-96) He also supports [public safety reform](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Police_reform_in_the_United_States "Police reform in the United States") and a $30 [minimum wage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minimum_wage_in_the_United_States "Minimum wage in the United States") by 2030.[[93]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-97) His platform called for tax increases on corporations and on those earning above $1[[94]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-98)
For most of the primary campaign, Mamdani trailed former New York governor [Andrew Cuomo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Cuomo "Andrew Cuomo") in polling. He and Cuomo raised similar amounts of money, but his donor base was considerably larger than Cuomo's.[[95]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-99) A poll taken shortly before the June 24, 2025, [primary election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Primary_election "Primary election") showed that Mamdani had caught up to Cuomo.[[96]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-100) [First-choice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ranked_voting "Ranked voting") results on election night showed Mamdani had a large lead over Cuomo,[[97]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-101)[[98]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-102) who conceded the race that evening.[[99]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-103) His polling margin was increased by [ranked choice voting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Instant-runoff_voting "Instant-runoff voting"), particularly because he and third-favored candidate [Brad Lander](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brad_Lander "Brad Lander") cross-endorsed each other by asking their voters to rank the other candidate second.[[100]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-104)[[101]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-105)[[102]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-106)[[103]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-107) Mamdani and [Michael Blake](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Michael_Blake_\(politician\) "Michael Blake \(politician\)") also cross-endorsed each other a few days later.[[104]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-BlakeMamdani-108) On June 16, _[The New York Times](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")_ editorial board advised voters not to rank Mamdani while criticizing Cuomo.[[105]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-109)
On July 1, after the New York City Board of Elections released its ranked-choice ballot tabulation, the Associated Press announced Mamdani had won the primary.[[106]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-110) It was considered a major upset.[[107]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-111) A July 2025 poll indicated a shift in [Jewish American](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jewish_American "Jewish American") political attitudes, with 43% of Jewish New Yorkers and 67% of Jewish voters under 44 planning to support Mamdani—levels of support suggesting waning attachment to traditional [pro-Israel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pro-Israel "Pro-Israel") politics. But many Jewish leaders and voters remained critical of him.[[108]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Yachot-2025-112) Mamdani's campaign launch drew media attention for its use of [Bollywood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bollywood "Bollywood") songs and cultural references, which aimed to engage younger and immigrant voters.[[109]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-113)[[110]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-114)
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/28/20250926New_York%2C_Estados_Unidos-_Reuni%C3%B3n_bilateral_con_el_candidato_a_la_Alcald%C3%ADa_de_Nueva_York%2C_Zohran_Mamdani_-_Juan_Cano09326.jpg/250px-20250926New_York%2C_Estados_Unidos-_Reuni%C3%B3n_bilateral_con_el_candidato_a_la_Alcald%C3%ADa_de_Nueva_York%2C_Zohran_Mamdani_-_Juan_Cano09326.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:20250926New_York,_Estados_Unidos-_Reuni%C3%B3n_bilateral_con_el_candidato_a_la_Alcald%C3%ADa_de_Nueva_York,_Zohran_Mamdani_-_Juan_Cano09326.jpg)Mamdani with Colombian President [Gustavo Petro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gustavo_Petro "Gustavo Petro") in New York City, September 26, 2025
During the primary, Mamdani's campaign was supported by the _New Yorkers for Lower Costs_ super PAC, which spent approximately $1.3 million supporting him and opposing [Cuomo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Cuomo "Andrew Cuomo") before the primary and raised an additional $1 million afterward. The super PAC received $100,000 in contributions from the Unity and Justice Fund in May and June 2025. Cuomo accused Mamdani of accepting "dirty money", saying the Unity and Justice Fund was tied to the [Council on American-Islamic Relations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Council_on_American-Islamic_Relations "Council on American-Islamic Relations").[[111]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-115)
After Mamdani won the primary, criticisms of and attacks against him used racist, [xenophobic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xenophobic "Xenophobic"), and [Islamophobic tropes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamophobic_tropes "Islamophobic tropes"), particularly references to the [9/11 attacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/9/11_attacks "9/11 attacks") and terrorism.[[112]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-auto2-116)[[113]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-auto3-117) These criticisms and attacks came from across the political spectrum, and sparked concern and debate over [Islamophobia in mainstream American politics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Islamophobia_in_mainstream_American_politics "Islamophobia in mainstream American politics").[[114]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-auto4-118)[[115]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-auto5-119)[[116]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Fayyad_Vox-120)[[117]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Saad_Post-121) The success of Mamdani's campaign was attributed in part to his use of social media to reach potential voters, especially younger voters.[[118]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-122)[[119]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-123)
In early November, President [Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") threatened to withdraw federal funding for New York City should Mamdani be elected mayor.[[120]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-124) Mamdani was elected mayor on November 4, 2025.[[121]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Decision_Desk_HQ-125)[[122]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-NBC_News-126)[[123]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-AP_News-127) He is the city's youngest mayor since 1892,[[124]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-128) as well as the city's first [Asian American](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asian_Americans "Asian Americans") and Muslim mayor.[[125]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-The_Statesman-2025-129)[[126]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-The_Washington_Post-2025-130) He is also the first foreign-born mayor since [Abraham Beame](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abraham_Beame "Abraham Beame"), who was born in the [United Kingdom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom").[[127]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-131)[[128]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-132)
### Mayor-elect and transition
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c8/Trump_and_Mamdani_meeting_3.jpg/250px-Trump_and_Mamdani_meeting_3.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Trump_and_Mamdani_meeting_3.jpg)Mamdani's meeting with president [Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") at the [White House](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/White_House "White House"), November 21, 2025
On November 5, 2025, the day after the election, Mamdani announced the first members of his mayoral transition team, with Elana Leopold as executive director, and co-chairs [Maria Torres-Springer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maria_Torres-Springer "Maria Torres-Springer"), the former first deputy mayor; [Lina Khan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lina_Khan "Lina Khan"), the former chair of the [Federal Trade Commission](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_Trade_Commission "Federal Trade Commission"); and nonprofit executives [Melanie Hartzog](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melanie_Hartzog "Melanie Hartzog") and Grace Bonilla.[[129]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-133) On November 10, he announced his selection of former first deputy mayor [Dean Fuleihan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dean_Fuleihan "Dean Fuleihan") as his first deputy, and his former Assembly chief of staff and campaign manager [Elle Bisgaard-Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elle_Bisgaard-Church "Elle Bisgaard-Church") as his Chief of Staff.[[130]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-134) He faced criticism for selecting Catherine Almonte Da Costa as director of appointments after past antisemitic comments of hers resurfaced; Da Costa resigned one day after her appointment.[[131]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-135)[[132]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-136)
Mamdani met with President Trump at the [White House](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/White_House "White House") on November 21. A spokesperson said the discussion would focus on public safety, economic security, and affordability.[[133]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-137)[[134]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-138) After the meeting, Trump praised Mamdani and said they "agree on a lot more than I would have thought", following months of mutual criticism.[[135]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-139)[[136]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-140)
Mamdani's mayoralty was expected to be the city's 111th, but a December 2025 review of city archives established that the enumeration of New York City mayors had omitted [Matthias Nicoll](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Matthias_Nicoll "Matthias Nicoll")'s second mayoralty, and that Mamdani would therefore be the city's 112th mayor.[[137]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-BerginGothamist-141)[[138]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-HonanCity-142)
### Tenure
Main article: [Mayoralty of Zohran Mamdani](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayoralty_of_Zohran_Mamdani "Mayoralty of Zohran Mamdani")
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/20/Zohran_Mamdani_swearing_in%2C_2026.png/250px-Zohran_Mamdani_swearing_in%2C_2026.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Zohran_Mamdani_swearing_in,_2026.png)Mamdani being ceremonially sworn in as mayor by Bernie Sanders with Mamdani's wife Rama Duwaji in the background  
| External videos |  
| --- |  
|  [![video icon](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/Nuvola_apps_kaboodle.svg/20px-Nuvola_apps_kaboodle.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Nuvola_apps_kaboodle.svg) [Mayor-Elect Mamdani Swearing-In, January 1, 2026](https://www.c-span.org/program/public-affairs-event/mayor-elect-mamdani-swearing-in/670975), [C-SPAN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/C-SPAN "C-SPAN")  |  
|  [![video icon](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/Nuvola_apps_kaboodle.svg/20px-Nuvola_apps_kaboodle.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Nuvola_apps_kaboodle.svg) [Mayor Zohran Mamdani Inauguration Ceremony, January 1, 2026](https://www.c-span.org/program/public-affairs-event/mayor-zohran-mamdani-inauguration-ceremony/670976), [C-SPAN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/C-SPAN "C-SPAN")  |  
Mamdani's mayoralty began on January 1, 2026. New York Attorney General [Letitia James](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Letitia_James "Letitia James") administered the oath of office in the abandoned [City Hall subway station](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/City_Hall_station_\(IRT_Lexington_Avenue_Line\) "City Hall station \(IRT Lexington Avenue Line\)") just after midnight.[[139]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-143) Mamdani took the oath on the [Quran](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quran "Quran"), making him the first mayor of the city to do so. He used two copies of the book, his grandfather's and [Arturo Alfonso Schomburg](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arturo_Alfonso_Schomburg "Arturo Alfonso Schomburg")'s.[[140]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-144) A public inauguration was held in the afternoon, at which Mamdani was sworn in by [Bernie Sanders](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bernie_Sanders "Bernie Sanders").[[141]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-145)[[142]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-146) Speakers and performers at the inauguration included Sanders, [Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexandria_Ocasio-Cortez "Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez"), [Javier Muñoz](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Javier_Mu%C3%B1oz_\(actor\) "Javier Muñoz \(actor\)"), [Lucy Dacus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lucy_Dacus "Lucy Dacus"), and [Mandy Patinkin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandy_Patinkin "Mandy Patinkin").[[143]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-147)
Mamdani's first act as mayor, immediately after taking the oath of office, was to appoint transportation consultant, educator, and former [NYC Department of Transportation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_Department_of_Transportation "New York City Department of Transportation") director of capital planning and project management [Mike Flynn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mike_Flynn_\(transportation_official\) "Mike Flynn \(transportation official\)") as his Department of Transportation commissioner.[[144]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-148) His first [executive order](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Executive_order "Executive order") was to revoke all executive orders his predecessor [Eric Adams](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eric_Adams "Eric Adams") made after he was [indicted](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Investigations_into_the_Eric_Adams_administration#Indictment "Investigations into the Eric Adams administration") on bribery charges on September 25, 2024.[[145]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-149)[[146]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-150)[[147]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-151)[[148]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-152)
One week after his inauguration, Mamdani and Governor [Kathy Hochul](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kathy_Hochul "Kathy Hochul") announced a plan to expand free and low-cost childcare in New York City, with the aim to eventually provide universal free childcare.[[149]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-153) One of Mamdani's first major challenges as mayor was managing the effects of a [January 2026 winter storm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/January_23%E2%80%9327,_2026_North_American_winter_storm "January 23–27, 2026 North American winter storm"), which killed 14 people in New York City.[[150]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-154)[[151]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-155)[[152]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-156)
#### Gracie Mansion bombing attempt
Main article: [2026 New York City bombing attempt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_New_York_City_bombing_attempt "2026 New York City bombing attempt")
On March 7, 2026, a [homemade explosive device](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Improvised_explosive_device "Improvised explosive device") was thrown into the crowd of an anti-Islam protest organized by far-right activist and [January 6 United States Capitol attack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/January_6_United_States_Capitol_attack "January 6 United States Capitol attack") rioter [Jake Lang](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jake_Lang "Jake Lang") outside the mayor's official residence, [Gracie Mansion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gracie_Mansion "Gracie Mansion").[[153]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-157) Police investigated the attack as an "act of ISIS-inspired terrorism". Two men, Emir Balat and Ibrahim Kayumi, were taken into custody,[[154]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-:0-158) and the [U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/U.S._Attorney_for_the_Southern_District_of_New_York "U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York") brought federal charges against them.[[155]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-159) Mamdani condemned the protest, calling it "appalling" and a display of anti-Muslim bigotry rooted in racism,[[156]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-RichardLuscombe-160) and also called Lang a "[white supremacist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/White_supremacist "White supremacist")".[[157]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-MamadaniTwitter-161)[[158]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-162) He added that the "right to peaceful protest is sacred" and "does not belong only to those we agree with".[[154]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-:0-158)

Mamdani identifies as a [democratic socialist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_socialist "Democratic socialist"),[[67]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Mamdani-2021-70)[[159]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-163) and is a member of the [Democratic Socialists of America](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_Socialists_of_America "Democratic Socialists of America").[[79]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Sterne-2024-83)[[160]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Fitzsimmons-20252-164) Described as a [progressive](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Progressivism_in_the_United_States "Progressivism in the United States") and [left-wing populist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Left-wing_populist "Left-wing populist"),[[161]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-165)[[162]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-166) his policies have been characterized as [center-left](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Centre-left_politics "Centre-left politics")[[163]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-167) and [left-wing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Left-wing "Left-wing").[[164]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-168) His economic platform centers on equity and affordability—supporting debt relief for [taxi medallion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taxi_medallion "Taxi medallion") owners,[[165]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-169) stronger [rent control](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rent_control "Rent control"), tenant protections, and a Social Housing Development Agency to build 200,000 affordable units.[[166]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Braun-20252-170)[[167]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-171) In [New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"), he advocates raising the local [minimum wage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minimum_wage "Minimum wage") to $30 by 2030,[[168]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-172) implementing higher taxes on corporations and high-income earners to fund free tuition at [CUNY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CUNY "CUNY") and [SUNY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/SUNY "SUNY"), [universal childcare](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universal_childcare "Universal childcare"), [city-owned grocery stores](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/City-owned_grocery_stores "City-owned grocery stores"), and [free public transit](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Free_public_transit "Free public transit"), while cutting taxes for [outer-borough](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boroughs_of_New_York_City#Background "Boroughs of New York City") homeowners and reforming New York's property tax system.[[e]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-178) Mamdani has cited [sewer socialism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sewer_socialism "Sewer socialism"), a democratic socialist movement centered in [Milwaukee, Wisconsin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milwaukee,_Wisconsin "Milwaukee, Wisconsin") in the early 20th century, as an inspiration for improving public infrastructure in New York City.[[174]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-179)
Mamdani supported [Proposal 1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_New_York_Proposal_1 "2024 New York Proposal 1")—a successful 2024 ballot measure that banned discrimination based on ethnicity, gender identity, disability, and reproductive rights—and backs [single-payer healthcare](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Single-payer_healthcare "Single-payer healthcare") through the [New York Health Act](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_Health_Act "New York Health Act").[[175]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-180)[[176]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-181)[[177]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-182)[[178]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-183) He argues that "dignified work, economic stability, and well-resourced neighborhoods" prevent harm better than policing, proposing a civilian Department of Community Safety to handle mental health crises and community outreach.[[179]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-184) He has voiced support for [defunding the NYPD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Defunding_the_police "Defunding the police") and accused the department of [racism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Police_racism "Police racism"), for which he later apologized. As a mayoral candidate, he instead emphasized building a cooperative relationship with the department on violent crime prevention while reducing the involvement of police in other matters.[[180]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-185)[[181]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-186)[[182]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-187)
Mamdani has condemned dictatorships in [Venezuela](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Venezuela "Venezuela") and [Cuba](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cuba "Cuba") while criticizing US sanctions on those countries.[[183]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-188) After [the U.S. captured Nicolas Maduro and his wife](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_strikes_in_Venezuela "2026 United States strikes in Venezuela"), Mamdani called the action "a violation of federal and international law".[[184]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-189)[[185]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-190) In January 2026, Mamdani criticized the [Iranian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iran "Iran") regime's [violence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Iran_massacres "2026 Iran massacres") against [anti-government protesters](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025%E2%80%932026_Iranian_protests "2025–2026 Iranian protests").[[186]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-191) He has denounced Indian prime minister [Narendra Modi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Narendra_Modi "Narendra Modi") as a "war criminal"[[187]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-192)[[188]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-193)[[189]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-194) and remains a critic of Israel and the [Gaza genocide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaza_genocide "Gaza genocide"),[[190]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-195)[[191]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-196)[[192]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-197) describing its policies as [apartheid](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_apartheid "Israeli apartheid")[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Glueck-202522-198) and supporting the movement to [boycott, divest from, and sanction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boycott,_Divestment_and_Sanctions "Boycott, Divestment and Sanctions") the country.[[194]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-199) He co-sponsored the "Not on our dime!" bill to stop New York charities from funding [Israeli settler violence](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_settler_violence "Israeli settler violence").[[195]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-:02-200) After the [October 7 attacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/October_7_attacks "October 7 attacks"), Mamdani mourned all victims, condemned [Hamas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hamas "Hamas")'s attacks as war crimes,[[196]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-201)[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Glueck-202522-198)[[197]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kornbluh-2025b22-202) and called for both sides to "lay down their arms" while advocating a permanent [ceasefire](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calls_for_a_ceasefire_during_the_Gaza_war "Calls for a ceasefire during the Gaza war"),[[198]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-203) the end of [Israeli occupation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Israeli_occupation "Israeli occupation"), and the end of [ethnocratic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ethnocratic "Ethnocratic") policy in favor of "equal rights for all".[[199]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-204)[[193]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Glueck-202522-198)[[200]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-205) He said he would attempt to honor the [International Criminal Court](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Criminal_Court "International Criminal Court")'s warrants for [Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Criminal_Court_arrest_warrants_for_Israeli_leaders "International Criminal Court arrest warrants for Israeli leaders") and [Russian president Vladimir Putin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Criminal_Court_arrest_warrants_for_Russian_leaders "International Criminal Court arrest warrants for Russian leaders") if they visit New York City.[[201]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-206)
Mamdani connects environmental justice to social equity.[[202]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-207) He opposed expansion of the gas-fired [Astoria Energy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charles_Poletti_Power_Project "Charles Poletti Power Project") power plant,[[203]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-208)[[204]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-209) supports the All-Electric Buildings Act and [congestion pricing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congestion_pricing_in_New_York_City "Congestion pricing in New York City"), and proposed a "Green Schools for a Healthier New York City" plan to retrofit schools with [solar panels](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solar_panels "Solar panels"), create green schoolyards, and establish resilience hubs.[[205]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Sim%C3%B5es-20252-210)[[206]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-211)[[207]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-212) His platform includes universal [pre-K](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pre-K "Pre-K"), [baby baskets](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maternity_package "Maternity package") for new families, defending Hasidic [yeshivas](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yeshivas "Yeshivas")' autonomy,[[208]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Kornbluh-2025a-213) expanding sanctuary protections for immigrants, making New York City an [LGBTQ](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LGBTQ "LGBTQ")+ sanctuary city with an Office of LGBTQIA+ Affairs, and eliminating bus fares through expanded [MTA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metropolitan_Transit_Authority_\(New_York\) "Metropolitan Transit Authority \(New York\)") funding and free transit initiatives.[[f]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-219)
After the deaths of [Renée Good](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ren%C3%A9e_Good "Renée Good") and [Alex Pretti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Killing_of_Alex_Pretti "Killing of Alex Pretti"), who were both killed by federal border patrol agents in January 2026, Mamdani took a strong stance against [ICE](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ICE "ICE"). He called for the organization's abolition, described Good's and Pretti's killings as murders, and blamed [Donald Trump](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump")'s "year of cruelty" for Good's death.[[214]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-220) In February 2026, Mamdani signed an executive order requiring ICE agents to get judicial warrants in New York City.[[215]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-221)
## Personal life
[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/Zohran_K._Mamdani_and_his_Wife.png/250px-Zohran_K._Mamdani_and_his_Wife.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Zohran_K._Mamdani_and_his_Wife.png)Mamdani and Rama Duwaji at Mamdani's mayoral inauguration
Mamdani is a [dual citizen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dual_citizen "Dual citizen") of Uganda and the United States; he was [naturalized](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naturalization#United_States "Naturalization") in the latter country in 2018.[[216]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-222) He follows the [Twelver](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twelver_Shi%27ism "Twelver Shi'ism") branch of [Shia Islam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shia_Islam "Shia Islam").[[36]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Shoki-2020-39)[[217]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-223) In 2021, Mamdani met animator and illustrator [Rama Duwaji](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rama_Duwaji "Rama Duwaji") through [Hinge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hinge_\(app\) "Hinge \(app\)"), an online dating application.[[218]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Pazmino-2025-224)[[219]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Hasan-2025-225) They became engaged in October 2024,[[218]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Pazmino-2025-224) held a private [_nikah_ ceremony](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marriage_in_Islam#Nikah_ceremony "Marriage in Islam") two months later in [Dubai](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dubai "Dubai"),[[220]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-226) and were married in February 2025 in a [civil ceremony](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Civil_ceremony "Civil ceremony") at [New York City Hall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_Hall "New York City Hall");[[221]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-227) they also had a ceremony in Uganda in July 2025.[[219]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Hasan-2025-225) The couple resided in Mamdani's legislative district of [Astoria, Queens](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Astoria,_Queens "Astoria, Queens"), near [Steinway Street](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steinway_Street "Steinway Street"),[[14]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Jung-2025-17)[[219]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-Hasan-2025-225)[[222]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-228) until they moved into [Gracie Mansion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gracie_Mansion "Gracie Mansion").[[223]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-229)
Besides English, Mamdani can speak at least five other languages with varying degrees of proficiency: [Hindi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hindi "Hindi"), [Swahili](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Swahili "Swahili"), [Luganda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luganda "Luganda"), Spanish, and Arabic.[[224]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-230)[[225]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-231)[[226]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-232) He is a [fan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arsenal_F.C._supporters "Arsenal F.C. supporters") of the [English football](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Premier_League "Premier League") team [Arsenal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arsenal_F.C. "Arsenal F.C.")[[227]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-233)[[228]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-234) and the [New York Knicks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_Knicks "New York Knicks") basketball team, whose games he has attended as part of past campaigns,[[229]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-235) and also follows [cricket](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cricket "Cricket")[[230]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-236) and [professional wrestling](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Professional_wrestling "Professional wrestling").[[231]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-237) He is a shareholder of the Spanish soccer team [Real Oviedo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Real_Oviedo "Real Oviedo"), having been one of 20,000 people to purchase shares in the club in 2012 as part of a fundraising drive to stave off bankruptcy.[[232]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-238)[[233]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-239)
## In popular culture
Mamdani was [Google](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Google "Google")'s top "People" trending search for 2025. His [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia "Wikipedia") article was among the 10 most-read in 2025.[[234]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-ZohranMamdaniMostSearchedMayor-240)
In the [51st season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live_season_51 "Saturday Night Live season 51") of _[Saturday Night Live](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saturday_Night_Live "Saturday Night Live")_ , [Ramy Youssef](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramy_Youssef "Ramy Youssef") portrayed Mamdani in a sketch based on the [second debate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election#Debates "2025 New York City mayoral election") of the [2025 New York City mayoral election](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_New_York_City_mayoral_election "2025 New York City mayoral election") _._[[235]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-241)

**Zohran Mamdani** ([Democratic Party](https://ballotpedia.org/Democratic_Party)) is the [Mayor of New York](https://ballotpedia.org/New_York,_New_York). He assumed office on January 1, 2026. His current term ends on January 1, 2030.
Mamdani (Democratic Party, Working Families Party) ran for election for [Mayor of New York](https://ballotpedia.org/New_York,_New_York). He won in the general election on [November 4, 2025](https://ballotpedia.org/Mayoral_election_in_New_York,_New_York_\(2025\)). He advanced from the Democratic primary on [June 24, 2025](https://ballotpedia.org/Mayoral_election_in_New_York,_New_York_\(2025\)). 
Mamdani announced that he was running for New York City mayor in October 2024.[[1]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bowdoin-1) In the [Democratic primary](https://ballotpedia.org/Mayoral_election_in_New_York,_New_York,_2025_\(June_24_Democratic_primary\) "Mayoral election in New York, New York, 2025 \(June 24 Democratic primary\)"), he defeated former governor [Andrew Cuomo](https://ballotpedia.org/Andrew_Cuomo "Andrew Cuomo") and nine other candidates, receiving 56.4% of the vote after three rounds of [ranked-choice voting](https://ballotpedia.org/Ranked-choice_voting "Ranked-choice voting"). In the [2025 general election](https://ballotpedia.org/Mayoral_election_in_New_York,_New_York_\(2025\) "Mayoral election in New York, New York \(2025\)"), he received 50.9% of the vote, defeating Cuomo, who ran as an independent, and [Curtis Sliwa](https://ballotpedia.org/Curtis_Sliwa "Curtis Sliwa") (R). Mamdani is the first Muslim and the first South Asian person to serve as mayor of New York City. [[2]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-2)
Mamdani was born in Kampala, Uganda, and moved to New York City with his family when he was seven years old. He attended Bronx High School of Science and went on to earn a bachelor’s degree in Africana Studies from Bowdoin College in 2014.[[1]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bowdoin-1)
In college, Mamdani cofounded his school's first Students for Justice in Palestine chapter and worked for _The Bowdoin Orient,_ the college's student newspaper and literary magazine.[[1]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bowdoin-1)[[3]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-assemblybio-3) After graduating, Mamdani worked as a foreclosure prevention housing counselor in Queens.[[1]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bowdoin-1)
Mamdani was first elected to the [New York Assembly](https://ballotpedia.org/New_York_State_Assembly "New York State Assembly") in [2020](https://ballotpedia.org/New_York_State_Assembly_elections,_2020 "New York State Assembly elections, 2020"). He defeated ten-year incumbent [Aravella Simotas](https://ballotpedia.org/Aravella_Simotas "Aravella Simotas") 51.2% to 48.6% in the Democratic primary and was uncontested in the general election.[[3]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-assemblybio-3) According to _Time Magazine,_ Mamdani's main focuses as a state legislator were housing and transportation reform.[[4]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-4)
Mamdani completed [Ballotpedia's Candidate Connection](https://ballotpedia.org/Ballotpedia%27s_Candidate_Connection "Ballotpedia's Candidate Connection") survey in 2020. As part of his response to the question "What areas of public policy are you personally passionate about?," Mamdani wrote, "I know that we'll never win the rights we all deserve - rights to healthcare, housing, education, nutrition, childcare, and public power - without a grassroots movement of the working class rising up to demand them." 
Mamdani describes himself as a Democratic socialist. In a 2020 campaign video, he said, "I’m a Democratic socialist, and what socialism means to me is a commitment to dignity, a state that provides whatever is necessary for its people to live a dignified life."[[5]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-5)
During his mayoral campaign, Mamdani said he wanted to "freeze the rent for all stabilized tenants," "permanently eliminate the fare on every city bus," "implement free childcare for every New Yorker aged 6 weeks to 5 years," and "create a network of city-owned grocery stores."[[6]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-6)

## Biography
Zohran Kwame Mamdani was born in Kampala, Uganda.[[7]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bio-7)[[8]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bio2-8) Mamdani graduated from Bronx High School of Science. He earned a bachelor's degree in Africana studies from Bowdoin College. Mamdani's career experience includes working as a foreclosure prevention housing counselor.[[8]](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#cite_note-bio2-8)

Expand all | Collapse all
[](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#collapse-bio-84047-2020-05-09)
I was born and raised in Kampala, Uganda, and grew up in New York City after my family moved here when I was seven years old. I graduated from Bronx Science and now work as a housing counselor, helping immigrant families facing eviction stay in the homes they worked their whole lives to earn. I am running for State Assembly because it's time to guarantee housing to all New Yorkers as a right, regardless of ability to pay. It's time to desegregate our schools, fully eliminate cash bail, ban solitary confinement, fund and fix the MTA, end workplace discrimination, and fight for social, racial, economic, and environmental justice for the many, not the few. 
[](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#collapse-key-message-84047-2020-05-09)
  * We need to stop treating housing as a commodity from which private developers can extract exorbitant profits, and start guaranteeing it as a human right for all.
  * Energy should be a public resource, not a for-profit commodity. We need to eliminate the profit motive from our energy system and empower the working class to reverse decades of disinvestment in frontline communities that have borne the brunt of environmental degradation and who are most imminently threatened by accelerating climate catastrophe.
  * I believe that people should not have to endure the violence and coercion of a criminal-legal system that props up the exploitation of the market by surveilling, caging, and killing those fighting to survive under capitalism.


[](https://ballotpedia.org/Zohran_Mamdani#collapse1-84047-2020-05-09)
The housing crisis we're facing in NY isn't restricted to low-income homeowners; it's impacting renters too. Half of all tenants in Astoria are spending a third or more of their income on rent. I spend more than half of my paycheck on rent, just like a quarter of my neighbors. Yet our landlords can refuse to renew our leases, evict us without good cause, and raise our rents whenever they like to prices we can't afford to pay.   
  
I know that we'll never win the rights we all deserve - rights to healthcare, housing, education, nutrition, childcare, and public power - without a grassroots movement of the working class rising up to demand them. Another world is possible, but only if we fight for it together.

A [scorecard](https://ballotpedia.org/State_legislative_scorecards) evaluates a legislator’s voting record. Its purpose is to inform voters about the legislator’s political positions. Because scorecards have varying purposes and methodologies, each report should be considered on its own merits. For example, an advocacy group’s scorecard may assess a legislator’s voting record on one issue while a state newspaper’s scorecard may evaluate the voting record in its entirety.
Ballotpedia is in the process of developing an encyclopedic list of published scorecards. Some states have a limited number of available scorecards or scorecards produced only by select groups. It is Ballotpedia’s goal to incorporate all available scorecards regardless of ideology or number.
[Click here](https://ballotpedia.org/State_legislative_scorecards) for an overview of legislative scorecards in all 50 states. To contribute to the list of scorecards, email suggestions to editor@ballotpedia.org.

# Zohran Mamdani Interview Transcript

### A transcript of The New York Editorial Board's interview with mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani.

[![New York Editorial Board's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4nQ1!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa879c8fa-b2bc-4cd8-9489-b41c01abe972_144x144.png)](https://substack.com/@nyeditorialboard)

[New York Editorial Board](https://substack.com/@nyeditorialboard)

Feb 02, 2025

55

6

9

Share

[![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!l-qL!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb6b9c25c-c626-46d5-9f36-cec41bd245f6_4032x3024.jpeg)](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!l-qL!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fb6b9c25c-c626-46d5-9f36-cec41bd245f6_4032x3024.jpeg)
